provenance.note:
"Запомни: Имена -- это не границы" in docx/png is indented, yet even in your "prose" section it doesn't have indent, is it ill-rendered prose or lineated?
And "Есть. Потому что всё моё творение" is glued to pancratius's question, despite they are clearly in separate paragraphs on png -- rendering bug or something more serious?
provenance.readjudication: Confirmed prose on the fixed render: the human's verdict in the winning pass was already prose; the note raised render questions (indent, glued paragraphs), not a lineated case. Section convention: the four gate paragraphs (8155/8160/8165/8170) and the surrounding Q&A flow are prose.

Confirmed prose on the fixed render: chapters 48-51 are a uniform short-prose-paragraph register (gold prose at 930/934, two human passes voted prose across the run); no convention tilt toward lineation.

provenance.note: Important: note that prose rendering or IR pipeline has a bug. "1. Вода" and other 3 items (4 total) all are on a new line on the docx screenshot, but on the "prose" render they are mangled into the whole paragraph.
provenance.readjudication: Render-bug verdict: the queue-audit lineated flip existed only to keep '3. Птица' on its own line under the prose-render mangling bug the note reports. The fixed render (f80ff63) renders header (sub 0) and body (sub 1) separately; the body is a flowing parable paragraph (fill 5.8). Sibling parable bodies 410.1/412.1/416.1 are gold prose. Section convention: prose.

provenance.note: The 2240-2244 is obviously lineated, independent of context. It's a single "punchline", with 2242-2243 even showing in-sentence break (which is super-strong indicator of lineation, if not wrapped). The wrapped one is also part of the same stanza/verse just because it's the same sentence (split by :), first of all, and second -- that it can't be extracted without breaking the idea communicated, would be "orphaned" after that. If something looks "orphaned" after being split, it's a strong indicator that it belongs somewhere. The only questionable thing here is 2247: it could be both prose and lineated, taken abstractly. BUT the tie is easy to fix: the whole book is lineated in original, so it would be inconsistent, thus lineated.
provenance.readjudication: Re-examined on the fixed render and KEPT lineated: the note's reasoning is bug-independent — 2240-2244 is an in-sentence-break punchline (a real lineation signal) and 2247 resolves by the human's stated convention tiebreak (the surrounding offer/parable lines 2237/2240-2244/2250-2256/2259 are all lineated). The fixed render's lone prose verdict at 2247 contradicts its own verdict on the identically-shaped 2237/2259 — flagged as an importer inconsistency, not truth.

provenance.note: Your prose renderer mangled it into a single p, so hard to say. Technically, it's lineated -- writted visually dense, without indents, so it's the most defensible option. But honestly — prose (bug-less, of course) would also work here just fine, assuming it matches other styling/conventions of a book or its section.
provenance.readjudication: Render-bug verdict: 'Your prose renderer mangled it into a single p, so hard to say'; the note explicitly allows prose 'assuming it matches conventions of the book or its section'. Fixed render: prose for the whole chapter-8 run. The run is dominated by multi-sentence wrapping paragraphs (55 fill 3.3, 57 fill 4.8) — the register of the chapter-lead prose paragraphs (43/50/62-63/69), not the short aphorism runs; 'visually dense' does not discriminate (the whole book is set dense, identical pPr on prose and lineated paragraphs). Section convention: prose.
